data.name: CF-WAF-Traversal — Path Traversal / LFI against shop.novamind.ai
data.description: Detects path-traversal and local-file-inclusion attempts on the NovaMind webstore asset/Workers routes (class_uid 4002, 'HTTP Requests'). Two independent, corroborating signals: (a) Cloudflare's WAF ML remote-code/traversal score (unmapped.WAFRCEAttackScore) cast with number() and in the high-confidence malicious band 1-20 (score is 1-99, LOWER=more malicious, NULL on benign), OR (b) canonical traversal markers in the request URI — '/etc/passwd', '../', or the URL-encoded '%2e%2e'. Groups by src_ip + host + country and reports which signal fired (waf_hits vs path_hits) plus a sample URI. Low false-positive: the ML band is a trained verdict and the literal markers virtually never appear in legitimate storefront traffic; combining them catches both WAF-scored and encoding-obfuscated probes. Any qualifying request triggers (rule threshold >0). MITRE ATT&CK T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application) and T1083 (File and Directory Discovery). Covers OneFlare scenario 03.

scheduledParams.query:
class_uid=4002 dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests'
| let src_ip     = src_endpoint.ip
| let host       = http_request.url.hostname
| let country    = src_endpoint.location.country
| let uri        = http_request.url.url_string
| let rce_score  = number(unmapped.WAFRCEAttackScore)
| let is_waf_rce = rce_score >= 1 AND rce_score <= 20
| let is_path    = uri contains:anycase("/etc/passwd") OR uri contains:anycase("../") OR uri contains:anycase("%2e%2e")
| filter (rce_score >= 1 AND rce_score <= 20) OR http_request.url.url_string contains:anycase("/etc/passwd") OR http_request.url.url_string contains:anycase("../") OR http_request.url.url_string contains:anycase("%2e%2e")
| group
    attack_requests = count(),
    waf_hits        = count(is_waf_rce),
    path_hits       = count(is_path),
    lowest_score    = min(rce_score),
    sample_uri      = any(uri),
    sample_ua       = any(http_request.user_agent),
    first_seen      = oldest(timestamp),
    last_seen       = newest(timestamp)
  by src_ip, host, country
| let detection_time = simpledateformat(last_seen, 'yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss z')
| sort -attack_requests
| columns detection_time, src_ip, host, country, attack_requests, waf_hits, path_hits, lowest_score, sample_uri, sample_ua, first_seen
| limit 100
